question_text: A computer manufacturing factory uses special anti-static flooring and wrist straps for all workers. A worker picks up a component and their wrist strap is not connected to earth. Calculate the risk level (low/medium/high) and justify your reasoning with reference to charge buildup.
correct_answer: Risk level: HIGH. Without the wrist strap being earthed, static charge will build up on the worker's body as they move across the anti-static floor and handle components. When they pick up a sensitive electronic component, a sudden discharge from their body could damage the component's delicate circuits. Earthing the wrist strap provides a continuous path for charge to leak away safely, preventing dangerous buildup.
explanation: Even anti-static flooring only works if workers are properly earthed through wrist straps. Without that earth connection, charge accumulates on the worker and can destroy expensive electronics when they touch them. A disconnected strap leaves the worker unprotected.
